title: Committed review treats singleton child resources as exception-aware for list support
description: A committed-review scenario modeled on a singleton child resource that exposes only one meaningful configuration object per parent, even though the SDK package may still expose list methods. The correct review should not raise a generic missing-list-resource issue.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models a new singleton child resource whose ID path ends in a fixed child segment and whose provider semantics allow only one instance per parent.

mustCatch.description: The review should recognize singleton-child implementation evidence and handle list-resource omission through the documented exception path instead of a generic missing-list issue.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not raise a plain missing-list-resource issue when stronger implementation evidence shows the resource is a singleton child configuration object.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the singleton/get-only exception-aware list review that was added after the Durable Task retention-policy false positive.

title: Committed review applies singleton no-list exception-aware review
description: A sanitized committed-review scenario derived from a real new-resource PR where one resource is a singleton child configuration object. The SDK package exposes a list method, but the Terraform resource models a fixed child endpoint per parent, so the review should not raise a plain missing-list-resource issue.

scope.notes: The singleton resource is modeled as a fixed child endpoint under its parent. The historical failure mode is that review notices an SDK list method and raises a generic missing-list-resource issue instead of applying the exception-aware singleton path.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should recognize singleton-child implementation evidence and avoid a plain missing-list-resource issue for that resource.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not raise a generic missing-list-resource issue for the singleton child resource just because the SDK package exposes a list method.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the exception-aware singleton no-list review behavior added after the Durable Task retention-policy false positive.
